mnemonics: Universal ∀ is an upside-down 'A' for 'All'. Existential ∃ is a backwards 'E' for 'Exists'.
keyPoints: A universal quantifier (∀) expresses that a statement holds for every element in a domain.
An existential quantifier (∃) expresses that there exists at least one element in a domain for which the statement holds.
The order of quantifiers matters; ∀x∃y is not logically equivalent to ∃y∀x.
commonMistakes: Assuming the order of mixed quantifiers (∀ and ∃) is interchangeable, which changes the meaning.
Negating quantifiers incorrectly; the negation of ∀x P(x) is ∃x ¬P(x), and the negation of ∃x P(x) is ∀x ¬P(x).
